page: Microsoft Azure D16nls_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the network-optimized Dnlv6 server family. It is provisioned via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity is constrained to 32.0 GB, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage options.

Benchmark data positions the D16nls_v6 in the average performance tier for most standard workloads, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1793.59 and a multi-core score of 13869.2. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to a top-tier text generation speed of 366.1 tokens per second during LLM inference with a small 135M parameter model. Memory bandwidth tests show 1073.33 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 120.65 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

The primary tradeoff of the D16nls_v6 is its low memory density and lack of local storage, which limits its utility for memory-intensive databases or large-scale local data caching. However, the dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon cores and network-optimized design offer qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. This configuration is suitable for network-optimized applications, web serving, small-scale LLM inference, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D16nls_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the network-optimized Dnlv6 family, powered by a 16-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for general compute, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 13869.2 and a static web serving throughput of 25.91 GB/sec. However, its support for Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) enables top-tier text generation speeds of 366.1 tokens per second for small LLM models. This server is suited for network-optimized applications, web serving, and compute-bound tasks with low memory requirements.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs at 4.2 GHz, 32 GB RAM, and no local storage, suited for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16nls_v6 is a 16-vCPU network-optimized VM with 32 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz.
tagline: A network-optimized, low-memory compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors with advanced matrix extensions for efficient vector operations.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a low 2.0 GB per core ratio
No local storage, requiring remote Premium Storage configurations
Intel AMX support delivering top-tier small LLM text generation speeds
Suited for network-optimized workloads, web serving, and light inference
